title: Hillrom Progressa Hospital Bed - Bed Won’t Move / Functions Intermittently
description: Guide for troubleshooting a Hillrom Progressa hospital bed that fails to respond, moves intermittently, or stops functioning.

steps.title: Verify Power Connection
steps.instructions: Confirm the bed is plugged into a live outlet or that its battery is charged. Check that the bed’s power cord is intact and fully seated. Why: Intermittent function is often caused by loose connections or depleted batteries.

steps.title: Check Bed Control Handset and Panel
steps.instructions: Inspect the handset for visible damage, frayed wires, or stuck buttons. Test all functions to see if some movements work while others don’t. Why: A malfunctioning handset can mimic pump failure.

steps.title: Inspect Fuses and Circuit Breakers
steps.instructions: Locate and check the bed’s internal fuses or reset any circuit breakers on the bed. Why: A blown fuse can prevent the pump from running or limit certain functions.

steps.title: Observe Mechanical Movement
steps.instructions: Ensure no bed rails, linens, or accessories are obstructing actuator movement. Check that wheels are properly locked/unlocked depending on intended movement. Why: Mechanical obstructions can prevent the bed from moving even if the pump is working.

steps.title: Test Battery (if applicable)
steps.instructions: If the bed has a battery pack, verify charge using the built-in indicator. Swap with a known-good battery if available. Why: Weak batteries often cause intermittent operation or slow movement.

steps.title: Listen for Pump Motor Activation
steps.instructions: Press a function button and listen for any motor sound. No sound may indicate electrical/pump failure. Why: Confirms whether the issue is electrical or mechanical.

steps.title: Inspect Wiring and Connections
steps.instructions: Check cables between the pump, handset, and motor assembly for loose or disconnected plugs. Why: Disconnected or damaged wires are a common cause of intermittent operation.

CCR.Complaint: Bed fails to respond to handset commands; functions intermittently.
CCR.Cause: Bed confirmed plugged in; handset buttons tested; no visible obstructions; fuses intact; pump motor silent.
CCR.Resolution: Bed removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Motor activation attempted, no sound heard
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Hillrom Progressa Bed Scale Inaccurate or Won’t Zero
description: Guide for troubleshooting inaccurate or non-zeroing scales on Hillrom Progressa hospital beds.

steps.title: Verify Bed Placement
steps.instructions: Ensure the bed is fully on a flat, hard surface. Uneven flooring or partially on a rug can affect the scale reading.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions
steps.instructions: Remove any items under or near the bed wheels or frame that may interfere with the scale sensors.

steps.title: Confirm Bed is Empty
steps.instructions: No mattress accessories, trays, or patient equipment should be on the bed during zeroing.

steps.title: Engage Zeroing Function
steps.instructions: Press the bed’s scale “Zero” or “Tare” button according to the bed’s user interface. Observe if the scale completes the zeroing process.

steps.title: Check Wheel Locks
steps.instructions: Ensure all bed wheels are fully locked. Bed movement during weighing can cause unstable readings.

steps.title: Inspect Display and Connections
steps.instructions: Confirm the display panel is properly seated and free of error messages. Check cables connecting the scale sensors to the control module for loose connections.

steps.title: Try a Known Weight
steps.instructions: Place a known weight (e.g., a calibration weight or similar) on the bed to verify scale function.

CCR.Complaint: Bed scale shows inaccurate readings or fails to zero.
CCR.Cause: Bed on uneven surface; zeroing fails; wheel locks engaged; connections verified but no improvement.
CCR.Resolution: Bed removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Floor type and condition under the bed

title: Hillrom Progressa Air Mattress Not Inflating / Pressure Alarms
description: Troubleshoot air mattress inflation issues and pressure alarms on Hillrom Progressa hospital beds with external checks and safe escalation.

steps.title: Verify Bed Power
steps.instructions: Ensure the bed is plugged into a functional outlet. Check the power switch on the bed’s control panel. Why: The mattress pump requires stable power; intermittent or no power prevents inflation.

steps.title: Check Control Panel Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm the bed’s mode is set correctly (e.g., therapy mode vs. standard). Verify that the correct patient weight and pressure settings are selected. Why: Incorrect settings can prevent the pump from inflating or trigger alarms.

steps.title: Inspect Mattress Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure all air hoses are securely connected to the mattress ports. Look for kinks, pinches, or obstructions in the tubing. Why: Even a small leak or disconnected tube can prevent proper inflation.

steps.title: Examine Mattress for Leaks or Damage
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the mattress surface and seams for punctures or tears. Perform a gentle press on the mattress while listening for hissing sounds. Why: Leaks prevent the mattress from maintaining pressure and trigger alarms.

steps.title: Check Pump and Hose Function
steps.instructions: Verify that the pump is running when inflation is initiated. Listen for abnormal sounds like grinding or irregular motor operation. Swap hoses with a known working mattress if available. Why: Ensures the pump is capable of delivering air and the hose is not faulty.

steps.title: Reset the Bed System
steps.instructions: Power off the bed, wait 30–60 seconds, and power it back on. Why: Clears transient faults or software glitches in the control system.

CCR.Complaint: Air mattress not inflating; low-pressure alarm displayed.
CCR.Cause: Hose connections loose and kinked; no power interruption detected.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected and straightened hoses; verified bed powered on; mattress inflated properly; alarm cleared.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested with a known device

title: Hillrom Progressa Bed - Side Rail Controls Not Responding
description: Guide for troubleshooting unresponsive side rail controls on a Hillrom Progressa bed, focusing on external checks before internal repair.

steps.title: Ensure the Bed Is Powered
steps.instructions: Verify the bed is plugged into a live outlet. Check that the main power switch on the bed (usually near the footboard) is in the ON position. Side rail controls rely on the bed’s main power; a disconnected or switched-off power source will render them inactive.

steps.title: Check the Battery Backup (if equipped)
steps.instructions: If the bed uses a battery backup, confirm the battery is charged. A completely drained battery can prevent the side rails from responding.

steps.title: Inspect the Control Cable Connection
steps.instructions: Side rail controls typically connect via a cable to the bed frame. Ensure the connector is fully seated and not damaged. Look for visible wear, bent pins, or frayed wires. Loose or damaged connections are a common cause of unresponsive controls.

steps.title: Test the Nurse/Bedside Control Panel
steps.instructions: Attempt to operate the bed using the central control or nurse handset. If the bed responds here but not the side rail, the issue is isolated to the side rail control.

steps.title: Clean the Side Rail Buttons
steps.instructions: Dust or debris can interfere with button contact. Wipe the controls gently with a damp cloth (no solvents). Physical contamination can prevent electrical contact inside the control buttons.

steps.title: Check for Lockout Mode
steps.instructions: Some Progressa beds have a side rail lockout feature. Verify lockout is not engaged; consult bed labeling or quick guide indicators.

steps.title: Restart the Bed Electronics
steps.instructions: If safe, power the bed down for 30 seconds, then power back on. Observe if side rail controls respond after reboot.

CCR.Complaint: Side rail controls do not respond when pressed; patient cannot adjust bed.
CCR.Cause: Control cable fully seated but side rail buttons remain unresponsive; central control responds normally.
CCR.Resolution: Bed powered down and re-checked; if still unresponsive, removed from service and labeled Out of Service; service request submitted.

title: Hillrom Progressa Hospital Bed – Brake or Steering System Faults
description: Guide for addressing brake or steering faults on a Hillrom Progressa bed, focusing on external causes first.

steps.title: Verify Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Ensure the bed is not occupied or the patient has been safely transferred if troubleshooting requires moving the bed. This prevents accidental injury during manipulation of brakes or wheels.

steps.title: Check Brake Engagement
steps.instructions: Inspect each wheel to ensure brakes are fully engaged. Manually press the brake pedals and attempt to gently move the bed. Uneven engagement may indicate a misaligned brake or mechanical obstruction.

steps.title: Inspect Wheels and Casters
steps.instructions: Look for debris, hair, or bedding caught in the casters. Check that all wheels rotate freely when brakes are released. A jammed wheel can cause steering resistance and false brake fault alerts.

steps.title: Check Steering Lock / Directional Caster Function
steps.instructions: For beds with directional casters, ensure they pivot freely. Verify that the steering lock mechanism (if present) is fully disengaged.

steps.title: Examine Electronic Brake Sensor (if equipped)
steps.instructions: If the bed has electronic brake sensors, confirm the connectors at the wheels are seated properly. Look for visible damage to wiring harnesses or sensors.

steps.title: Test Bed on Flat Surface
steps.instructions: Place the bed on a flat, even surface. Test braking and steering while observing for inconsistencies or fault messages. Faults that only occur on certain floor types may indicate caster or sensor sensitivity.

steps.title: Cycle Power (Electronic Beds Only)
steps.instructions: Disconnect power briefly (unplug AC or remove battery if safe). Reconnect and observe if faults clear. This resets sensor systems without requiring internal repair.

CCR.Complaint: Bed reports “Brake Fault” on control panel and is difficult to maneuver.
CCR.Cause: Observed debris in rear caster and partial brake pedal engagement; sensors appeared functional after visual inspection.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared debris, fully engaged brakes, and cycled bed power; fault cleared. Bed returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Number of wheels with obstruction or resistance
Visual condition of brake pedals and casters
Any unusual sounds when engaging brakes

title: Hillrom Centrella Smart+ Hospital Bed - Nurse Call Integration Failure
description: Guide to troubleshoot nurse call integration issues on Hillrom Centrella Smart+ beds, focusing on external checks and system verification.

steps.title: Verify Power to Bed
steps.instructions: Ensure the bed is connected to a live AC outlet. Check the bed’s main power indicator LED. Why: Nurse call integration will not work if the bed has no power.

steps.title: Check Bed Network/Communication Cables
steps.instructions: Inspect cables connecting the bed to the nurse call interface (RJ45, serial, or proprietary connector). Ensure cables are firmly seated, undamaged, and routed properly. Why: Loose or damaged cables are a common cause of communication failure.

steps.title: Verify Nurse Call System Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the nurse call system is powered and operational. Check for any active system alarms or faults that could prevent communication. Why: Bed-side signals rely on a functioning nurse call system.

steps.title: Check Bed Settings for Nurse Call Integration
steps.instructions: On the bed’s control panel, navigate to the settings menu for nurse call or alert configuration. Confirm integration is enabled and configured for the correct room or station. Why: Incorrect configuration can prevent alerts from being sent.

steps.title: Swap with a Known-Good Bed or Room
steps.instructions: Move the bed to a room with a functioning nurse call system (if feasible) or replace it temporarily with a bed known to work. Why: Isolates whether the issue is the bed or the nurse call interface.

steps.title: Inspect for Software Updates or Bed Firmware Issues
steps.instructions: Check Hillrom documentation for the latest firmware that addresses nurse call integration. Why: Some integration issues are resolved via software updates.

steps.title: Test Bed Functions Not Related to Nurse Call
steps.instructions: Operate bed adjustments and bed exit sensors. Why: Confirms that bed electronics and sensors are functional before escalating.

CCR.Complaint: Bed is not triggering nurse call alerts.
CCR.Cause: Bed exit sensors functional; integration cables secure; nurse call system operational; bed settings confirmed.
CCR.Resolution: Bed removed from service and sent for manufacturer evaluation.

title: Hillrom Centrella Smart+ Hospital Bed - Exit False Alarms
description: Guide for troubleshooting false bed exit alarms on the Hillrom Centrella Smart+ bed, focusing on external checks before internal faults.

steps.title: Verify Bed Position and Brakes
steps.instructions: Ensure the bed is fully lowered and brakes are engaged. Some sensors require proper bed leveling and brake engagement to function correctly.

steps.title: Check Mattress and Sensor Pad Placement
steps.instructions: Confirm the mattress is correctly seated and any integrated sensor pads are properly positioned. Misaligned pads or folds can trigger false alarms.

steps.title: Inspect Bed Exit Sensor Connections
steps.instructions: Check that cables from the bed exit sensor are securely connected to the control module. Look for visible damage, pinched wires, or loose connectors.

steps.title: Confirm Control Module Settings
steps.instructions: Access the bed’s control panel and verify bed exit sensitivity settings. Adjust sensitivity or reset to default if values seem incorrect. Overly sensitive settings can trigger alarms unnecessarily.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Bed
steps.instructions: Turn the bed off, wait 10–15 seconds, then power back on. Resets sensors and software, clearing transient false alarms.

steps.title: Check Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure there’s no interference from equipment under or near the bed (IV poles, cables, sheets obstructing sensors). Obstructions can trigger sensors falsely.

CCR.Complaint: Nurse reports bed exit alarm sounds while patient remains in bed.
CCR.Cause: Bed exit sensor pad misaligned; control panel sensitivity set too high.
CCR.Resolution: Repositioned sensor pad, adjusted sensitivity to default, and power cycled bed. Alarm now operates normally.

helpfulDetails: Bed fully lowered and brakes engaged

title: Hillrom Centrella Smart+ Hospital Bed - Scale Inaccuracy
description: Guide for troubleshooting inaccurate or drifting weight readings on the Hillrom Centrella Smart+ bed, focusing on external checks before internal repair.

steps.title: Verify Bed is on a Stable Surface
steps.instructions: Ensure the bed is on a level, hard floor. Uneven surfaces can affect scale readings. Why it matters: Scale sensors are sensitive to tilt and surface irregularities.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions Under the Bed
steps.instructions: Remove floor mats, cables, or items under the bed that could interfere with weight sensors.

steps.title: Confirm Bed is Unloaded
steps.instructions: Ensure no patient, linens, or equipment are on the bed when performing a test reading.

steps.title: Inspect Cables and Connectors
steps.instructions: Check power cables and data connections to the scale module for secure attachment and visible damage.

steps.title: Test with a Known Weight
steps.instructions: Place a calibration weight or a patient with a known, stable weight on the bed. Compare the reading to the known weight to identify the magnitude of the inaccuracy.

steps.title: Check for Software or Firmware Errors
steps.instructions: Access the bed interface to verify no scale-related error messages are displayed. Restart the bed system if possible and re-test the scale.

steps.title: Re-zero the Scale (If Allowed)
steps.instructions: Follow the manufacturer’s external procedure to zero or tare the scale. Retest with a known weight to confirm improvement.

CCR.Complaint: Bed reports patient weight inconsistently; sudden jumps observed.
CCR.Cause: Bed placed on uneven surface; cables checked and secure; zeroing did not correct reading.
CCR.Resolution: Bed removed from service; labeled Out-of-Service; sent for manufacturer repair.

helpfulDetails: Observed weight readings vs. expected weight
Bed serial number and last calibration date
Any recent maintenance or software updates

title: Hillrom Centrella Smart+ Hospital Bed - Drive Motor Malfunction
description: Troubleshoot situations where the Hillrom Centrella Smart+ bed drive motor fails to operate correctly, including unusual noises, movement failure, and alarms.

steps.title: Verify Power Supply
steps.instructions: Check that the bed is plugged into a functional outlet. Ensure that the bed’s main power switch is ON. Confirm battery charge if applicable. Why: Motors will not operate without proper voltage; power issues are a common cause of drive failure.

steps.title: Inspect Cables and Connectors
steps.instructions: Examine power and motor control cables for damage, kinks, or loose connections. Ensure connectors are fully seated at both the motor and control interface. Why: Loose or damaged cables can prevent motor operation or cause intermittent function.

steps.title: Check the Bed Control Interface
steps.instructions: Attempt to move the bed using the control panel and/or pendant. Verify that no error codes or motor lockouts are displayed. Why: If controls are not sending commands properly, the motor will not operate.

steps.title: Test Bed Movement Without Load
steps.instructions: Move the bed without a patient to see if the motor functions normally. Observe for unusual sounds, sluggish movement, or failure to start. Why: This isolates mechanical binding or excessive load from motor malfunction.

steps.title: Inspect for Mechanical Obstructions
steps.instructions: Check wheels, tracks, or drive components for debris, jams, or physical damage. Ensure the brakes are fully released during the test. Why: Mechanical interference can prevent the motor from moving the bed.

steps.title: Reset the Bed System
steps.instructions: Power down the bed, wait 30–60 seconds, then power it back on. Test motor function again. Why: Temporary electronic faults may be cleared with a system reset.

CCR.Complaint: Bed drive motor will not move the bed forward or backward; motor produces a humming noise.
CCR.Cause: Power verified, cables intact, control panel functional, no mechanical obstructions; motor failed to engage during testing.
CCR.Resolution: Bed removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for manufacturer repair.

helpfulDetails: Control panel response and error codes

title: Hillrom Centrella Smart+ Hospital Bed - Control Touchscreen Failure
description: Guide to troubleshoot unresponsive or intermittently failing touchscreens on Hillrom Centrella Smart+ beds.

steps.title: Verify Bed Power
steps.instructions: Ensure the bed is plugged into a working outlet and turned on. Observe if power indicators illuminate; a powered touchscreen cannot function without bed power.

steps.title: Check Battery Status (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Some beds rely on internal batteries for touchscreen function. Confirm battery charge level; low battery may prevent touchscreen response.

steps.title: Inspect Touchscreen Surface
steps.instructions: Clean the touchscreen with a soft, lint-free cloth and mild disinfectant. Remove any protective films, moisture, or debris that could interfere with touch recognition.

steps.title: Confirm Connections
steps.instructions: Verify that all visible cables connecting the touchscreen to the bed’s main control unit are securely seated. Look for loose connectors, bent pins, or damaged cables.

steps.title: Perform a Bed Reset
steps.instructions: If safe, power cycle the bed: turn off, wait 30 seconds, then turn back on. This can clear minor software glitches affecting the touchscreen.

steps.title: Observe for Software Errors
steps.instructions: Check for error codes on the bed display or any indicator lights. Document any codes for service escalation if needed.

steps.title: Test Alternate Control (if available)
steps.instructions: Use handset or side controls to verify if bed functions respond. If side controls work but touchscreen does not, issue is isolated to the touchscreen interface.

CCR.Complaint: Touchscreen unresponsive; buttons do not register inputs.
CCR.Cause: Bed powers on; side controls functional; touchscreen remains unresponsive.
CCR.Resolution: Performed cleaning, verified power and connections, performed reset; no change. Bed removed from service and sent for repair.

title: Hillrom TotalCare Hospital Bed - Therapy Surface or Air Mattress Not Inflating
description: Identifies causes of therapy surface or air mattress inflation failure, including power, connections, pump, and air hose issues before assuming mattress failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely transferred to another bed or mattress. Never attempt mattress repair with a patient in place.

steps.title: Check Bed Power
steps.instructions: Verify the bed is plugged into a functional outlet. Confirm the bed’s power switch is on. Observe for any status lights on the bed controller or pump. Expected Outcome: Bed and pump are powered; lights indicate readiness.

steps.title: Inspect Mattress Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure all air hoses from the mattress to the pump are fully connected and free of kinks or bends. Expected Outcome: Secure connections with no visible damage.

steps.title: Examine Air Pump Operation
steps.instructions: Turn the pump on and listen for motor operation or airflow. Check for unusual noises, overheating, or vibration. Expected Outcome: Pump runs smoothly, producing airflow.

steps.title: Check Mattress Valves and Ports
steps.instructions: Verify that valves on the mattress are closed and ports are unobstructed. Expected Outcome: No air leaks; valves properly seated.

steps.title: Try Alternate Mattress or Pump
steps.instructions: Swap with a known working mattress or pump if available. Expected Outcome: Confirms whether the issue is with the mattress or pump system.

CCR.Complaint: Bed therapy surface will not inflate despite pump activation.
CCR.Cause: Air hose connection loose and mattress valve partially open.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected hose, secured valve; mattress inflated successfully.

helpfulDetails: Unusual pump noises or vibration noted

title: Hillrom TotalCare Hospital Bed - Hi-Lo or Trendelenburg Function Failure
description: Identifies causes of bed height or tilt movement failure, including power, connection, actuator, and control issues before assuming internal motor or board failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely transferred to another bed or positioned securely. Never attempt movement troubleshooting with an active patient in the bed.

steps.title: Verify Power Supply
steps.instructions: Check that the bed is plugged into a live outlet. Inspect the cord for visible damage. Expected outcome: Bed should show power indicator lights on the control panel.

steps.title: Check Battery Backup
steps.instructions: If using battery backup, confirm battery is charged. Swap with a known good battery if possible. Expected outcome: Bed should operate normally on battery power.

steps.title: Inspect Control Panel & Handset Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure handsets or side rail panels are securely connected. Look for loose cables or damaged connectors. Expected outcome: Buttons respond and indicator lights function.

steps.title: Test Movement Functions Individually
steps.instructions: Attempt hi-lo and Trendelenburg movements separately. Listen for actuator noises or observe any movement. Expected outcome: Motors engage smoothly without unusual sounds.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions or Mechanical Locking
steps.instructions: Ensure nothing is physically blocking actuator movement. Inspect caster brakes and bed frame alignment. Expected outcome: Bed moves freely without mechanical restriction.

steps.title: Swap Actuator Cables if Accessible
steps.instructions: If multiple actuators share connectors, try swapping cables to isolate a faulty actuator. Expected outcome: Fault follows the actuator or cable.

CCR.Complaint: Bed will not raise, lower, or tilt into Trendelenburg.
CCR.Cause: Handset and power verified; actuator shows no response when tested.
CCR.Resolution: Bed removed from service and sent to repair for actuator evaluation.

title: Hillrom TotalCare Hospital Bed - Bed Exit Alarm False Alarms
description: Identifies causes of false bed exit alarms, including sensor misalignment, mattress placement, wiring, or configuration issues before assuming sensor failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely out of the bed or seated in a chair. Never troubleshoot alarms while the patient is at risk of falling.

steps.title: Check Mattress and Bed Positioning
steps.instructions: Ensure the mattress is correctly seated and the bed is fully flat or in the correct configuration. Misaligned mattresses can trigger false alarms. Expected outcome: alarm stops if mattress alignment was the issue.

steps.title: Inspect Bed Exit Sensor Pads
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat the sensor pads, checking for proper placement and no wrinkles or folds. Look for dirt or debris under pads. Expected outcome: pad properly seated, alarm returns to normal.

steps.title: Verify Sensor Connection Cables
steps.instructions: Check that all sensor cables are firmly connected to the bed’s control panel. Loose or partially connected cables can cause intermittent alarms. Expected outcome: secure connections, alarm functions correctly.

steps.title: Check Control Panel Settings
steps.instructions: Access the bed’s nurse interface and review bed exit alarm settings, including sensitivity and delay. Restore to default if uncertain. Expected outcome: alarm responds appropriately to patient movement.

steps.title: Observe for External Interference
steps.instructions: Ensure there are no additional weight-bearing objects on the bed (linens, devices, other equipment) that might trigger the sensor. Expected outcome: alarm only activates with patient movement.

CCR.Complaint: Bed exit alarm triggers without patient movement.
CCR.Cause: Sensor pad misaligned and mattress slightly off-center.
CCR.Resolution: Mattress and sensor pad repositioned; alarm tested and functioning normally.

helpfulDetails: Bed labeled Out of Service if unresolved

title: Hillrom TotalCare Hospital Bed - Scale Drift or Inaccuracy
description: Addresses inaccurate or drifting weight readings, including power, connection, sensor, and environmental factors before assuming internal scale failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the bed is unoccupied or the patient is safely moved to another bed. Never troubleshoot scales with a patient on the bed.

steps.title: Check Bed Level and Placement
steps.instructions: Ensure the bed is on a firm, level surface. Uneven floors can cause scale drift or false readings. Expect stable weight readings after leveling.

steps.title: Inspect Power and Connections
steps.instructions: Verify the bed is properly plugged in and power is stable. Loose or intermittent connections can affect scale electronics. Expected outcome: no intermittent power warnings.

steps.title: Examine Scale Sensors and Housings
steps.instructions: Check under-bed load cells and wiring for visible damage, debris, or misalignment. Ensure sensors are seated correctly. Outcome: sensors clean, aligned, and unobstructed.

steps.title: Verify Mattress and Accessories
steps.instructions: Remove or reposition mattress and any overlay. Improper placement can interfere with load cell readings. Outcome: scale reading stabilizes.

steps.title: Check Calibration Status
steps.instructions: If the bed allows electronic calibration, verify it has not been disrupted. Perform calibration per manufacturer guidelines if safe. Outcome: accurate weight reading restored.

steps.title: Observe for Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure no items (IV poles, carts) are in contact with the bed frame. Vibrations or floor movement can affect readings. Outcome: scale readings stabilize when environment controlled.

CCR.Complaint: Bed scale drifting; weight readings inconsistent.
CCR.Cause: Load cell misalignment and loose mattress placement identified.
CCR.Resolution: Bed leveled, sensors inspected and reset, mattress repositioned; scale reading stabilized.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: in service or sent for repair

title: Hillrom TotalCare Hospital Bed - Nurse Call Integration Failure
description: Addresses inability of the bed to communicate with nurse call systems, including wiring, connectors, configuration, and power issues before assuming internal electronics failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely in bed and alert staff to potential communication delay. Do not perform troubleshooting that could disrupt patient care.

steps.title: Verify Bed Power
steps.instructions: Check that the bed is plugged in and powered on. Confirm indicator lights on the bed control panel are normal. Without proper power, nurse call signals cannot transmit.

steps.title: Check Nurse Call Cable Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect the nurse call interface cable for secure connections at both the bed and wall jack. Look for bent pins, damaged plugs, or loose wiring. Proper connection ensures signal transmission.

steps.title: Inspect Bed Side Rails and Modules
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat any removable side rail modules that may carry nurse call wiring. Misaligned modules can interrupt signals to the nurse call system.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Wall Jack / System
steps.instructions: If possible, connect the bed to a verified functional nurse call jack or system. If the nurse call activates, the issue is likely the original wall or system connection.

steps.title: Check Bed Configuration Settings
steps.instructions: Review the bed’s integrated settings for nurse call functionality. Ensure the bed is configured to send alerts to the correct nurse call line. Incorrect settings can prevent alerts from triggering.

steps.title: Observe Alarm and Indicator Behavior
steps.instructions: Trigger a test nurse call from the bed. Confirm the system receives the alert. Note any error messages or LED behavior for documentation.

CCR.Complaint: Bed nurse call system not notifying staff when patient initiates call.
CCR.Cause: Loose connection at bed nurse call interface module discovered during inspection.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated module and verified communication with nurse call system; bed returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Test nurse call signal successfully received

title: Hillrom CareAssist Hospital Bed - Actuator Failure or Slow Movement (Hi-Lo, Backrest, Knee Gatch)
description: Addresses slow, uneven, or non-functioning bed movements due to power, control, or connection issues before assuming actuator failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely transferred to another bed or properly supported. Do not troubleshoot bed movement with a patient at risk.

steps.title: Verify Bed Has Power
steps.instructions: Check that the bed is plugged into a working outlet and the power indicator is on. Why: Low or no power can cause slow or non-responsive actuators.

steps.title: Check Battery Status
steps.instructions: Disconnect AC power and attempt movement using battery backup. Then reconnect AC power. Expected Outcome: Weak or failed batteries may allow limited or slow movement. If performance changes, battery may be degraded.

steps.title: Inspect Hand Control and Side Rail Controls
steps.instructions: Test all movement functions from both the hand pendant and side rail controls. Why: If one control works and the other does not, the issue is likely a control interface or cable—not the actuator.

steps.title: Check for Bed Lockout or CPR Mode Activation
steps.instructions: Ensure no movement lockouts are enabled and that the bed is not in CPR/emergency release mode. Expected Outcome: Locked-out functions will prevent actuator movement.

steps.title: Inspect for Obstructions or Mechanical Binding
steps.instructions: Visually inspect under the bed and around linkages for debris, bent components, or pinch points. Why: Mechanical resistance can mimic actuator failure or cause slow movement.

steps.title: Listen for Actuator Noise During Operation
steps.instructions: Attempt movement and listen for motor noise. Noise with no movement → possible mechanical or actuator issue. No noise → possible electrical or control issue.

steps.title: Check Actuator and Cable Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect accessible actuator cables and connectors for looseness, damage, or pinched wiring. Reseat connections if safe to do so. Expected Outcome: Loose connections can cause intermittent or failed movement.

steps.title: Test Individual Functions Separately
steps.instructions: Attempt hi-lo, backrest, and knee gatch independently. Why: A single failed actuator vs. multiple failures helps isolate whether the issue is localized or system-wide.

steps.title: Cycle the Bed Power
steps.instructions: Unplug the bed, wait 30 seconds, then reconnect power and retest. Why: Resets control electronics and may restore function if a control fault occurred.

CCR.Complaint: Bed backrest and hi-lo functions moving slowly and intermittently unresponsive.
CCR.Cause: Loose actuator cable connection identified at control box causing inconsistent power to actuator.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated and secured actuator connection, tested all bed movements, and confirmed normal operation.

helpfulDetails: Audible actuator noise present or absent
Any visible obstruction or mechanical binding
Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Hillrom CareAssist Hospital Bed - Bed Exit Alarm False Alarms
description: Addresses repeated or unexpected bed exit alarms caused by setup, mattress positioning, sensitivity settings, or connection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safe and attended to. Do not troubleshoot while relying on the alarm for fall prevention. Move the patient if needed.

steps.title: Confirm Alarm Mode and Status
steps.instructions: Verify the bed exit alarm is properly enabled and set to the correct mode (e.g., zone or sensitivity level). Incorrect mode can trigger alarms with normal patient movement.

steps.title: Check Mattress Placement and Type
steps.instructions: Ensure the mattress is properly seated and centered on the deck. Misaligned or non-compatible mattresses can interfere with pressure sensing and cause false alarms.

steps.title: Inspect for Bedding Interference
steps.instructions: Check for excess linens, pads, or items tucked under the mattress. These can shift weight distribution and trigger the alarm unexpectedly.

steps.title: Verify Patient Positioning
steps.instructions: Ensure the patient is positioned correctly within the detection zone. Patients near the edge or frequently repositioning can cause repeated alerts.

steps.title: Check Bed Exit Sensitivity Settings
steps.instructions: Adjust sensitivity to an appropriate level for the patient condition. High sensitivity may cause nuisance alarms with minimal movement.

steps.title: Inspect Sensor and Deck Area
steps.instructions: Look for visible damage, debris, or obstruction under the mattress or on the deck surface. Anything affecting pressure distribution can lead to false triggering.

steps.title: Cycle Bed Power
steps.instructions: Unplug the bed, wait 30 seconds, and restore power. This resets system logic and may clear sensor calibration issues.

steps.title: Check for Recent Mattress or Accessory Changes
steps.instructions: Confirm whether a different mattress, overlay, or accessory was recently installed. Non-OEM or incompatible accessories are a common cause of alarm issues.

steps.title: Test Alarm Functionality
steps.instructions: With the bed empty, activate the alarm and simulate patient movement. Alarm should trigger only when appropriate thresholds are met. If functioning normally after adjustments, return bed to service.

CCR.Complaint: Bed exit alarm alarming repeatedly without patient attempting to exit bed.
CCR.Cause: Mattress misalignment and high sensitivity setting causing improper pressure detection.
CCR.Resolution: Repositioned mattress, adjusted sensitivity, and verified proper alarm function.

helpfulDetails: Alarm mode and sensitivity level checked
Alarm tested with and without patient
Final device status (returned to service or removed)

title: Hillrom CareAssist Hospital Bed - Side Rail Control Panel Failure
description: Addresses unresponsive or partially working side rail controls caused by power, lockouts, cable issues, or panel failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safe and not dependent on the side rail controls. Move the patient if necessary before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Bed Has Power
steps.instructions: Check that the bed is plugged into a working outlet and the main power indicator is on. If no power is present, restore power and retest the panel. A dead panel may simply indicate loss of power.

steps.title: Check for Lockout/Disable Settings
steps.instructions: Inspect caregiver control panel or footboard controls for lockout settings. Ensure side rail controls are not disabled. Lockouts are a common cause of “failed” panels.

steps.title: Test All Side Rail Panels
steps.instructions: Check each side rail control panel individually. If only one panel is unresponsive, issue is likely isolated to that panel or its cable. If all panels fail, suspect power, communication, or control board issue.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the affected side rail panel: cracked buttons or housing, fluid intrusion or residue, loose or damaged mounting. Damage may indicate panel failure requiring replacement.

steps.title: Check Cable Routing at Hinge Points
steps.instructions: Inspect wiring where the side rail pivots and connects to the bed frame. Look for pinched, stretched, or severed cables. Movement over time commonly damages wiring in these areas. If damage is found, remove from service.

steps.title: Reseat Accessible Connections
steps.instructions: If accessible without disassembly, check and reseat connectors at the base of the side rail or junction points. Loose connections can cause intermittent or total failure. Retest after reseating.

steps.title: Cycle Bed Power
steps.instructions: Unplug the bed, wait 30 seconds, then restore power. This may reset control electronics. Retest panel function after reboot.

steps.title: Test Function from Alternate Controls
steps.instructions: Attempt the same functions using caregiver controls or other panels. If functions work elsewhere, confirms localized panel or wiring issue. If functions fail everywhere, broader system issue is likely.

steps.title: Evaluate for Intermittent Operation
steps.instructions: Operate the panel multiple times and observe: delayed response, inconsistent function, partial button failure. These symptoms often indicate internal panel wear or cable fatigue.

CCR.Complaint: Right-side rail control panel unresponsive; no bed movement from patient controls.
CCR.Cause: Side rail panel cable damaged at hinge point causing loss of signal.
CCR.Resolution: Removed bed from service and sent for side rail cable/panel replacement.

helpfulDetails: Visible damage to panel or cable
Final device status (in service or removed)

title: Hillrom CareAssist Hospital Bed - Scale Drift or Inaccuracy
description: Addresses inaccurate or drifting weight readings caused by mattress position, accessories, setup, or environmental factors before assuming internal scale failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely transferred to another bed or fully supported. Do not troubleshoot or zero the scale with a patient on the bed.

steps.title: Verify Bed Is on a Level Surface
steps.instructions: Ensure all four wheels are on a flat, even floor and brakes are engaged. Uneven surfaces can cause incorrect readings.

steps.title: Check for External Contact or Obstructions
steps.instructions: Ensure nothing is touching the bed (walls, IV poles, equipment, linens dragging on the floor). Even light contact can affect load cell readings.

steps.title: Remove All Extra Items from Bed
steps.instructions: Remove pumps, bags, extra linens, or equipment not normally part of the bed. Only the standard mattress and required accessories should remain.

steps.title: Inspect Mattress and Deck Position
steps.instructions: Ensure the mattress is properly seated and not bunched or overhanging. Verify the bed is in a neutral, flat position (not articulated).

steps.title: Check for Locked-Out or Improper Scale Settings
steps.instructions: Verify the scale is enabled and not locked out via control panel settings. Confirm units (lb/kg) are correct to avoid perceived inaccuracies.

steps.title: Perform a Scale Zero (Tare) Procedure
steps.instructions: With the bed empty and properly set up, zero the scale per normal operation. The scale should read zero consistently without drifting.

steps.title: Test with Known Weight
steps.instructions: Place a known test weight (e.g., 25 lb or 50 lb) evenly on the bed. Verify the reading is accurate and stable. If accurate → issue resolved. If drifting or incorrect → continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect Cables and Connections (External Only)
steps.instructions: Check visible scale-related cables under the bed for damage, pinching, or disconnection. Do not disassemble internal components.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Bed
steps.instructions: Unplug the bed, wait 30 seconds, and reconnect power. This can reset scale electronics and clear minor faults.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported bed scale showing inconsistent and drifting weight readings.
CCR.Cause: Bed was in contact with wall and had additional equipment affecting load distribution.
CCR.Resolution: Removed obstructions, cleared extra items, re-zeroed scale, and verified accuracy with test weight.

helpfulDetails: Bed on level surface and brakes engaged
Final device status (in service or removed)

title: Hillrom CareAssist Hospital Bed - Power Supply or Battery Backup Failure
description: Identifies causes of bed power loss or failure of battery backup, including outlet issues, power cords, fuses, and external connections before assuming internal electronics failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safely transferred to another bed or fully supported. Do not attempt power troubleshooting while the patient depends on bed functions.

steps.title: Verify AC Power Source
steps.instructions:
Check that the wall outlet is live using a verified device or outlet tester.
Expectation: Outlet is functional; if not, reset breaker or use a different outlet.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions:
Check the bed’s power cord for visible damage, kinks, or loose plugs.
Ensure connectors to the bed are fully seated.
Expectation: Secure, undamaged cord; loose or damaged cords may cause intermittent or total power failure.

steps.title: Check Fuses and Circuit Breakers on Bed
steps.instructions:
Locate bed’s internal fuses or circuit breakers per external access points (no disassembly required).
Expectation: Fuses intact; tripped breakers reset.

steps.title: Test Battery Backup
steps.instructions:
If the bed has a removable or built-in battery, verify it is installed correctly and shows charge.
Expectation: Bed functions on battery power; if not, battery may be discharged or failed.

steps.title: Power Cycle Bed
steps.instructions:
Disconnect AC power and battery (if applicable), wait 1–2 minutes, then reconnect.
Expectation: Bed powers on normally. This can reset minor control board errors without internal repair.

steps.title: Observe Indicator Lights and Bed Response
steps.instructions:
Confirm bed control panels, side rails, and actuators respond when power is applied.
Expectation: Lights illuminate and actuators operate; absence indicates persistent electrical issue.

CCR.Complaint: Bed fails to power on and battery backup does not operate.
CCR.Cause: Power cord, AC outlet, and battery checked; all appear functional, but bed remains unresponsive.
CCR.Resolution: Bed removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for manufacturer evaluation.

title: Hillrom VersaCare Hospital Bed - Hi-Lo Actuator Failure or Slow Movement
description: Troubleshooting hi-lo actuator failure or slow movement caused by power, control, or mechanical issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Move the patient to another bed before troubleshooting. Engage brakes and ensure the bed is stable.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to raise and lower the bed using all controls including side rails, footboard, and pendant. Observe for no movement, slow or uneven movement, or abnormal sounds.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Bed Power Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the bed is plugged into a known working outlet. Check power indicators and ensure the bed is not running on battery only.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions: Check for damage, loose connections, and ensure the cord is fully seated at the bed base.

steps.title: Check for Bed Lockout or Control Restrictions
steps.instructions: Verify no lockout functions are active and that hi-lo controls are enabled.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Obstructions
steps.instructions: Check under the bed for debris, linens, or damage that could restrict movement.

steps.title: Listen for Actuator Operation
steps.instructions: Listen for motor sounds, clicking, grinding, or complete silence while activating controls.

steps.title: Check Bed Weight Load
steps.instructions: Ensure the bed is not overloaded or unevenly loaded. Remove excess equipment if needed.

steps.title: Inspect Pendant and Control Cables
steps.instructions: Check for damaged or pinched cables and test with a known working pendant if available.

steps.title: Perform Power Reset
steps.instructions: Unplug the bed, wait 30–60 seconds, plug back in, and retest functionality.

CCR.Complaint: Bed will not raise or lower; hi-lo function unresponsive.
CCR.Cause: Hi-lo actuator not responding despite verified power and controls.
CCR.Resolution: Removed bed from service and sent to shop for actuator evaluation and repair.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (in service or removed)

title: Hillrom VersaCare Hospital Bed - Bed Exit Alarm False Alarms
description: Troubleshooting false bed exit alarms caused by sensitivity settings, patient movement, mattress issues, or sensor/calibration problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is safe and not attempting to exit the bed. If alarms are frequent or unreliable, place the patient on appropriate fall precautions.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Activate the bed exit alarm and observe when false alarms occur during patient repositioning, no patient in bed, or random activation without movement.

steps.title: Check Bed Exit Alarm Sensitivity Setting
steps.instructions: Review current sensitivity level (Low / Medium / High). Reduce sensitivity if set too high and match setting to patient size and mobility.

steps.title: Inspect Mattress Type and Placement
steps.instructions: Verify correct Hillrom-compatible mattress is installed. Ensure mattress is centered, has no gaps or shifting, and is proper thickness and type.

steps.title: Check for Objects or Weight on Bed
steps.instructions: Ensure no items such as blankets, equipment, or bed rails are affecting sensors or adding uneven weight.

steps.title: Verify Patient Positioning
steps.instructions: Confirm patient is centered on the bed and not sitting on the edge unless intended.

steps.title: Inspect Bed Frame and Deck Alignment
steps.instructions: Check for loose deck sections, misalignment, or mechanical instability.

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset
steps.instructions: Unplug bed from AC power, wait 30 seconds, reconnect, and reinitialize the bed exit alarm.

steps.title: Test Bed Exit Alarm Function
steps.instructions: With no patient, activate alarm and apply/remove pressure manually to observe proper activation timing and consistency.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reports bed exit alarm activating repeatedly without patient movement.
CCR.Cause: Sensitivity set too high combined with uneven mattress positioning causing false pressure readings.
CCR.Resolution: Adjusted sensitivity setting and repositioned mattress; verified normal alarm function.

helpfulDetails: Presence of extra items on bed

title: Hillrom VersaCare Hospital Bed - Scale Drift or Inaccurate Weight Readings
description: Troubleshooting inaccurate or drifting weight readings caused by setup issues, environmental factors, or calibration problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not rely on the bed scale for clinical decisions until accuracy is verified. If weight is critical, use an alternate scale.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Check current weight reading and compare to known or recent documented patient weight. Observe for gradual drift, sudden changes without movement, or inability to zero properly.

steps.title: Ensure Proper Bed Position and Setup
steps.instructions: Verify the bed is on a flat, level surface, all four wheels are in contact with the floor, and brakes are engaged.

steps.title: Check for External Interference
steps.instructions: Inspect for anything touching or resting on the bed such as IV poles, Foley bags touching the floor, or linens caught between the bed and wall.

steps.title: Remove Excess Items from Bed
steps.instructions: Remove extra linens, blankets, or equipment not part of normal patient setup.

steps.title: Zero the Scale (Tare) Properly
steps.instructions: With the bed empty or baseline items in place, use the scale zero/tare function and ensure no movement during zeroing.

steps.title: Reweigh the Patient
steps.instructions: After zeroing, return the patient to the bed if removed and allow the weight reading to stabilize.

steps.title: Check for Movement or Instability
steps.instructions: Observe if weight fluctuates when the patient moves or the bed is adjusted.

steps.title: Inspect Bed Frame and Scale Components (External Only)
steps.instructions: Visually inspect frame alignment, signs of damage, and loose components.

steps.title: Perform Power Reset
steps.instructions: Unplug the bed from AC power, wait 10–15 seconds, plug back in, and retest the scale.

steps.title: Verify Calibration Status (If Accessible)
steps.instructions: Check calibration status per facility protocol and compare with a known test weight if available.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported bed scale giving inconsistent and drifting weight readings.
CCR.Cause: External interference from Foley bag contacting floor and improper zeroing identified.
CCR.Resolution: Removed interference, re-zeroed scale, and verified stable and accurate readings.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (in service or removed)

title: Hillrom VersaCare Hospital Bed - Nurse Call Integration Failure
description: Troubleshooting nurse call integration failure, including cabling, wall jack, and configuration issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not rely on the bed’s nurse call integration for patient monitoring during troubleshooting. Ensure alternate monitoring (manual checks or working call device) is in place.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Activate the nurse call from the bed and confirm whether no signal is received, signal is intermittent, or only certain features fail.

steps.title: Check Nurse Call Cable Connection at Bed
steps.instructions: Inspect the nurse call cable connection at the bed, ensure it is fully seated, undamaged, and connected to the correct port. Reseat firmly.

steps.title: Inspect Nurse Call Cable and Facility Jack
steps.instructions: Trace the cable to the wall jack, check for damage or loose connections, and test with a known-good cable if available.

steps.title: Test Wall Jack / Nurse Call System
steps.instructions: Plug a known working device into the same wall jack to determine if the issue is with the bed/cable or facility system.

steps.title: Check Bed Settings and Configuration
steps.instructions: Verify nurse call integration is enabled, communication mode is correct, and bed exit alarm settings are properly configured.

steps.title: Test Bed Exit Alarm Integration
steps.instructions: Activate the bed exit alarm and confirm whether it triggers the nurse call system.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage to Bed Interface Port
steps.instructions: Check the interface port for bent pins, wear, fluid intrusion, or loose internal connections.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Bed
steps.instructions: Turn the bed off, disconnect power briefly, and restore power to reset communication systems.

CCR.Complaint: Bed nurse call not triggering at nurse station when activated.
CCR.Cause: Damaged nurse call cable preventing signal transmission.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced nurse call cable and verified proper operation at wall jack and nurse station.

helpfulDetails: Wall jack tested with known working device
Final device status (functional or removed from service)

title: Hillrom VersaCare Hospital Bed - Side Rail Control Panel Failure
description: Side rail controls not responding, intermittent button function, or complete panel failure due to cable, lockout, or power-related issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Ensure the patient is safe and the bed is in a stable position. If controls are needed, use alternate controls (footboard or caregiver pendant if available).

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Press multiple buttons on the affected side rail panel. Check if all buttons are unresponsive, only certain functions fail, or the opposite side rail works normally.

steps.title: Check for Lockout or Control Restrictions
steps.instructions: Inspect the caregiver control panel (usually at footboard). Verify that side rail controls are not locked out.

steps.title: Test Bed Power and System Functionality
steps.instructions: Confirm the bed is plugged into a known working outlet. Check for power indicators and test other bed functions (hi/lo, backrest, etc.).

steps.title: Inspect Side Rail Cable and Connection Points
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the cable running from the side rail to the bed frame. Look for pinched or damaged wiring, loose or disconnected connectors, and signs of wear near hinge points. Gently reposition the rail while testing buttons.

steps.title: Swap or Compare with Opposite Side Rail (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: If both rails are similar, compare function with the working side. If connectors are accessible and identical, swap connections (if safe and allowed).

steps.title: Check for Fluid Intrusion or Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the control panel for cracked buttons or housing, sticky or unresponsive keys, and signs of fluid ingress (cleaning solutions, spills).

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset
steps.instructions: Unplug the bed from AC power. Wait 30–60 seconds, then reconnect power.

CCR.Complaint: Side rail control panel unresponsive; unable to adjust bed from patient side.
CCR.Cause: Failed side rail control panel due to worn internal keypad/cable fatigue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed bed from service and sent for repair; confirmed issue isolated to side rail assembly.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (in service or removed)
